title: Saviour Like a Shepherd Lead Us
author: Dorothy A. Thrupp (attr.)
story: Published anonymously in Thrupp's 'Hymns for the Young.' The shepherd imagery weaves Psalm 23 and John 10 into a tender prayer for guidance.

lines: Savior, like a shepherd lead us, much we need Thy tender care
In Thy pleasant pastures feed us, for our use Thy folds prepare
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
Thou hast bought us, Thine we are
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
Thou hast bought us, Thine we are
We are Thine, Thou dost befriend us, be the guardian of our way
Keep Thy flock, from sin defend us, seek us when we go astray
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
Hear, O hear us when we pray
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
Hear, O hear us when we pray
Thou hast promised to receive us, poor and sinful though we be
Thou hast mercy to relieve us, grace to cleanse and pow'r to free
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
We will early turn to Thee
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
We will early turn to Thee
Early let us seek Thy favor, early let us do Thy will
Blessï¿½d Lord and only Savior, with Thy love our bosoms fill
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
Thou hast loved us, love us still
Blessï¿½d Jesus, blessï¿½d Jesus
Thou hast loved us, love us still
